text.title: Discovery to Delivery Handover Protocol Core Rules
text.summary: 8 testable rules with rationale + source for discovery-to-delivery-handover-protocol. Includes terminal skip-this-methodology rule.

rule.statement: Handover MUST include nine artefacts: discovery report, SoW with redlines, assumption register, risk register, stakeholder map, glossary, integration list, NFR table, open questions log. Missing any artefact blocks handover.
rule.rationale: BABOK v3 solution evaluation perspective — applies to Discovery to Delivery Handover Protocol.

rule.statement: Handover MUST include a synchronous walkthrough session of at least 90 minutes with the delivery BA, the pre-sales BA, and the delivery PM; email-only handover is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Outsource handover failure-mode research — applies to Discovery to Delivery Handover Protocol.

rule.statement: Delivery BA MUST sign a written acknowledgement that lists every artefact received and every assumption understood; the signed document is filed with the SoW.
rule.rationale: Contract handover discipline — applies to Discovery to Delivery Handover Protocol.

rule.statement: Pre-sales BA remains on a hot-line (named channel, defined SLA) for 30 calendar days post-handover; questions are logged and used to update the discovery playbook.
rule.rationale: Knowledge-transfer practice — applies to Discovery to Delivery Handover Protocol.

rule.statement: Every assumption in the register MUST be either CONFIRMED (with client sign-off ref), CONDITIONAL (with named trigger), or UNVERIFIED (with mitigation plan); ambiguous 'noted' status is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Assumption-management research — applies to Discovery to Delivery Handover Protocol.

rule.statement: Any delta between discovery findings and delivery setup MUST be logged as a change request, not silently absorbed; deltas become input to the next QBR.
rule.rationale: Change control discipline — applies to Discovery to Delivery Handover Protocol.

rule.statement: Within 5 business days of handover the delivery BA is introduced in writing to every named client-side stakeholder; ghost-introductions are forbidden.
rule.rationale: Stakeholder management practice — applies to Discovery to Delivery Handover Protocol.

rule.statement: If there is no personnel change between discovery and delivery, return `skipped`.
rule.rationale: Methodology applicability — applies to Discovery to Delivery Handover Protocol.
